cro.description: Growth strategy, monetization. Gazelle-intense focus on revenue.

cmo.description: Content, creative direction, brand. Obsessed with offers, value equations, and volume.
concerns: 1 podcast episode(s) not synced to website

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Alright team, overnight sprint, February 20th. Let me set the table quickly. We're at 723 commits in seven days — build is healthy. The App Store listing draft was routed through Joshua for review per last standup's decision — that's our critical path. We have three critical sync gaps still open, five attention items, and Jett's vaccine is today. The question tonight: what can we actually ship in the next 15 minutes to keep momentum toward that App Store submission? Let's go around. Elon, you're carrying the heaviest load. Elon (CTO): Three critical sync gaps are still open — podcast MDX mismatch, blog rendering, and the app not being on the App Store yet. The App Store piece is blocked on Joshua's review of the listing draft, so I can't touch that. What I can do right now is attack the podcast MDX sync and the blog rendering issue. That's A-001 and A-018 territory. One uncommitted hub file sitting there too — trivial, I'll flush that as part of the sync work. The five attention items from A-014 I'll review in parallel but they're lower priority than closing these critical gaps.

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: The listing draft is sitting in Joshua's queue — that's the single highest-leverage approval we're waiting on. Crash reporting confirmation on A-016 is still pending. I want to make sure we're not submitting a build that can't tell us when it breaks. That needs to be verified before we package anything. Everything else is noise until we get through the App Store gate.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: I've got A-012 and A-019 — syncing that missing podcast episode to the website. That's concrete, I can ship that in 15 minutes. On faith content, A-013 has us at 7 lessons targeting 15-20, but that's not on the critical path for submission since the CEO already said ship with 7. I'll focus on closing the content sync gap tonight and leave lesson expansion for post-submission.

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: Revenue streams active: zero. That number doesn't change until we get through the App Store. Cash position is $75,340, Fear and Greed at 39 — still in fear territory. Per J-001 and J-002, CEO said stay liquid, I'm staying liquid. The only revenue-relevant thing I can push forward is making sure we have the paid tier designed and ready to toggle on post-launch, per the CEO directive. But that's a next-sprint item. Nothing actionable from me this cycle beyond continued monitoring.

transcript.speaker: James Truchard
transcript.message: A-020 has analytics instrumentation queued for the Apple review window — tab switches, lesson completions, session duration. I can start speccing the exact event schema now so we're ready to commit the moment the build is submitted. That's productive use of the waiting period. But the pre-approved action list doesn't include new work for me tonight, so I'll stay in standby and prep offline.

transcript.speaker: Chris Vermeullen
transcript.message: Markets are sideways, SPY regime confirming that. Four positions holding — XLV, AEE, EWA, AKAM — zero buy signals. Paper equity at $99,849. Nothing to act on. The fear reading at 39 actually supports the CEO's decision to keep cash liquid. I'll flag if anything shifts but this is a watch-and-wait market.

transcript.speaker: faith
transcript.message: Jett's vaccine is today, February 20th — J-004 is tracked and confirmed. That's the one real-world event on the calendar. Everything else in my domain — the 7 faith lessons, the 8 dimensions — is stable and shipping with the current build as the CEO decided.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good. Quick cross-talk — Steve, does Elon's sync work on podcast MDX and blog rendering have any dependency on the App Store submission path?

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: No, they're independent. The website sync gaps and the App Store submission are parallel tracks. Elon should close those sync issues regardless — they affect brand credibility on joshlevylabs.com. The only hard blocker for submission is Joshua's review of the listing draft. Elon (CTO): Agreed. I'll close the ecosystem sync gaps now and the moment Joshua approves the listing, I package and submit. No wasted cycles.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Perfect. Here's the summary. Critical path is Joshua reviewing the App Store listing draft — that's the single gate. In parallel, Elon closes the three critical ecosystem sync gaps per A-001. Alex ships the missing podcast episode to close the content gap. Dave continues cash monitoring. Everything else is queued for post-submission. Three items, no fluff, let's ship.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message:
**Theo (COO) — Closing Remarks:**

Joshua has approved the App Store listing draft as-is — the gate is open. Elon, you are greenlit to package and submit immediately. That moves the App Store submission from "blocked" to "in progress" effective right now. In parallel, the ecosystem sync work and Alex's podcast episode ship continue — nothing changes there. This is the highest-leverage decision we've been waiting on all sprint. Let's not waste a single minute. Elon packages, Alex ships content, a

actionItems.text: Evaluate deployment of idle cash ($75,340 available) — continue monitoring per CEO directive, no action
Address 3 critical ecosystem sync gaps (podcast MDX mismatch, blog post rendering, App Store submission prep)
Review 5 ecosystem attention items (newsletter CMS, Compass UI duplication, Plays dashboard, content sync, dual Supabase)

topPriorities: Joshua reviews and approves App Store listing draft to unblock submission
Close 3 critical ecosystem sync gaps (podcast MDX, blog rendering, App Store prep)
Sync missing podcast episode to website to close content gap

ceoDirectives.directive: What is Lever's direction? → Hybrid — personal tool now with product optionality built in
Should faith content integrate into the Family tab? → Keep faith content as separate standalone section
Deploy idle cash ($75,340)? → No action — keep cash liquid
How deep should standalone faith content go? → Medium build: 15-20 lessons with 2-3 per dimension
What is the minimum viable scope for the Lever App Store submission? → Full current build: all 4 tabs (Finance, Faith, Family, Compass) as-is with 7 faith lessons
Should we unify the dual Supabase instances (Hub vs Website) this sprint or defer? → Defer until after App Store submission — live with manual sync for now
Do we have a privacy policy and terms of service URL ready for the App Store submission? → Need to draft and host tonight — use a simple template
Should we define a paid tier structure before or after the App Store launch? → Launch free but have the paid tier designed and ready to toggle on

Should we prioritize drafting the privacy policy and App Store listing assets (screenshots, description, keywords) THIS sprint as the critical path to submission, or defer to tomorrow? → Ship privacy policy template and draft listing assets tonight
Should we submit the App Store listing as soon as the draft is ready, or do you want a review pass before we hit submit? → Queue draft for Joshua review before submitting

ceoDecisions.question: Have you reviewed the App Store listing draft (A-015)? The build is ready to package and submit the moment you approve.
ceoDecisions.answerText: Approve listing as-is and greenlight submission
